Metropolitan Statistical Area in the United States

The **Sioux City metropolitan statistical area**, as defined by the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), is an area consisting of four counties in three states – [Iowa](/wiki/Iowa "Iowa"), [Nebraska](/wiki/Nebraska "Nebraska"), and [South Dakota](/wiki/South_Dakota "South Dakota"), anchored by the city of [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/United_States_Census,_2020 "United States Census, 2020"), the MSA had a population of 145,940.[[1]](#cite_note-1) [Plymouth County, Iowa](/wiki/Plymouth_County,_Iowa "Plymouth County, Iowa"), was removed from the definition of the MSA prior to the 2020 census.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

* [Plymouth County, Iowa](/wiki/Plymouth_County,_Iowa "Plymouth County, Iowa"), until 2020

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sioux_City_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Historical population

| Census | Pop. | Note | %± |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [1990](/wiki/1990_United_States_census "1990 United States census") | 115,018 |  | — |
| [2000](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census") | 143,005 |  | 24.3% |
| [2010](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census") | 168,825 |  | 18.1% |
| [2020](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census") | 145,940 |  | −13.6% |
| U.S. Decennial Census[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Plymouth County, Iowa](/wiki/Plymouth_County,_Iowa "Plymouth County, Iowa"), was removed from the MSA prior to the 2020 census. | | | |

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[4]](#cite_note-GR2-4) of 2000, there were 143,005 people, 53,586 households, and 36,735 families residing within the MSA. The racial makeup of the MSA was 87.40% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 1.58% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 1.54% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 2.31% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.04% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 5.19% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 1.93% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 10.17% of the population.

The median income for a household in the MSA was $39,084, and the median income for a family was $45,638. Males had a median income of $30,799 versus $22,162 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the MSA was $18,650.

The [Sioux City Human Rights Commission](/wiki/Sioux_City_Human_Rights_Commission "Sioux City Human Rights Commission") is an impartial governmental agency that works to protect the rights of the diverse population who [live](/wiki/Fair_housing "Fair housing") and [work](/wiki/Employment_discrimination "Employment discrimination") in Sioux City.

## Combined statistical area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sioux_City_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Combined statistical area")]

The **Sioux City–[Vermillion](/wiki/Vermillion,_South_Dakota "Vermillion, South Dakota") combined statistical area (CSA)** is made up of five counties in three states: Iowa, Nebraska, and South Dakota. The statistical area includes one [metropolitan area](/wiki/United_States_metropolitan_area "United States metropolitan area") and one [micropolitan area](/wiki/United_States_micropolitan_area "United States micropolitan area"). The CSA had a population population of 175,638 as of 2020.

* **Metropolitan statistical areas (MSAs)**
  + [Sioux City, Iowa](/wiki/Sioux_City,_Iowa "Sioux City, Iowa") ([Woodbury County, Iowa](/wiki/Woodbury_County,_Iowa "Woodbury County, Iowa"); [Plymouth County, Iowa](/wiki/Plymouth_County,_Iowa "Plymouth County, Iowa"); [Dakota County, Nebraska](/wiki/Dakota_County,_Nebraska "Dakota County, Nebraska"); [Dixon County, Nebraska](/wiki/Dixon_County,_Nebraska "Dixon County, Nebraska"); *[Wayne County, Nebraska](/wiki/Wayne_County,_Nebraska "Wayne County, Nebraska")*; and [Union County, South Dakota](/wiki/Union_County,_South_Dakota "Union County, South Dakota"))
* **Micropolitan statistical areas (μSAs)**
  + Vermillion ([Clay County, South Dakota](/wiki/Clay_County,_South_Dakota "Clay County, South Dakota"))

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Sioux_City_metropolitan_area&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Media")]

Sioux City, Iowa has been featured in an Independent Lens series documenting bullying.[[5]](#cite_note-GR4-5)

The Sioux City metropolitan area is also featured in the book 100 Things to Do in Sioux City & Siouxland Before You Die by Lindsay Hindman (Reedy Press, Sept. 2020)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
